calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-09-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PES-73-205402 The Estate of William J. Naughton.

Appearances: Paul Mori for and with William Naughton, Jr. and Peter Naughton in the William Naughton Estate. Paul Mori for and with Peter Naughton in the Helen Naughton Estate.

With regard to William Naughton's Estate, the Court directed Counsel to go back and delete all statutory fees from the time sheets and request extraordinary fees. Mr. Mori explained the extent of services he provided and the difficulty in communicating with his Client. The matter was continued to November 22, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. William Naughton, Jr. stated that he signed the declaration regarding the fees but that he was not approving them. The Court approved the accounting/fee request in Helen Naughton's Estate (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Monica Wiley. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Anasse.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-12-2007 Continued To Dec-28-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Nov-05-2007 Continued To Mar-03-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Called with case #205402. Appearances: Paul Mori for and with Peter Naughton and William Naughton, Jr. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA").

The Court made sure William Naughton was able to hear and or read the proceedings. Mr. Mori reported that he had attempted to file the status report, but was not able to, that the house at 251 Munich Street was completed, that the taxes for 2002 in the amount of $1,200.00 would be paid, that Ms. Dolch had records of the stocks and that the lost stock certificates were still being obtained. Mr. Ferdon suggested having the matter heard at another time that would better meet Mr. Naughton's needs. The matter was continued to March 3, 2008 for further status on the administration of both estates.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Felicia Smith

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-27-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Called with case number 205402. Stephen Gould appearing for and with special administrator Debra Dolch. Paul Mori appearing for and with William Naughton and Peter Naughton. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for the Public Administrator.
On Ms. Dolch's two accountings and request for fees, Mr. Ferdon objected to all fees at this stage. Mr. Gould objected to Mr. Ferdon's objections. The Public Administrator will not ask for fees. Mr. Mori did not object to Ms. Dolch's request for fees. Accounting is approved. Fees are submitted.
On the Petition for Instructions, the $750.00 is in Mr. Gould's trust account. He is instructed to split it into cost for each estate. Mr. Gould will prepare an order.
On the co-executors's status report, Court is approving reimbursement to Peter Naughton for funds he has advanced to complete the repair. Half to be charged to each estate. Mr. Mori will prepare an order.
On relieving Ms. Dolch as special administrator. There are claims still pending. Dividends checks are coming in. There may be more assets.
On William Naughton's petition for probate in the Helen estate, Mr. Ferdon objected to William's appointment. The PA will serve if it is in the best interest for Helen's estate. Mr. Mori will assist William Naughton to complete the estate. Mr. Naughton agreed to work with his counsel. Mr. Naughton has found the original Will. He has it with him in court. It appeared to be an original Will dated 7/23/1993. Court is appointing William Naughton as the executor with no bond. The Will is admitted today subject to Mr. Mori filing a declartion on the original Will. Mr. Mori will submit an order with his declaration.
Mr. Mori and Mr. Gould will work out the transition. Court is reinstating William and Peter as co-executors. Mr. Gould is instructed to file his original declaration.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Deborah Neville CSR# 9703. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Saephan.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jul-06-2007 Written Order Received

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-02-2007 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-02-2007 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-14-2007 Continued To Jul-02-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Called with case number 205402. Stephen Gould appearing for and with special administrator Debra Dolch. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. Paul Mori appearing for and with William and Peter Naughton. ADA Court Reporter is provided for William Naughton. Ms. Dolch has filed a status report. She has recovered about $1,000,000.00 in William's estate and there may be more monehy to come. In Helen's estate, she has recovered $20,000.00 cash and some stocks need to be recover. She is ready to file an accounting in William's estate. Court is prepared to admit Helen's Lost Will and appoint Debra Dolch as administrator with will annexed. Mr. Ferdon objected to the appointment. His office have not completed their investigations. He wanted to know what is the status of the house, filing of taxes and other assets. Mr. Mori also objected to Ms. Dolch's appointment. He will be filing an exparte petition for approval of repair contract. Mr. Ferdon questioned why the personal representatives don't have the authority to fix it. Both Mr. Mori and Mr. Ferdon objected to have Ms. Dolch's authority expanded to do the taxes. Mr. William Naughton has filed some taxes. Matter is continued to 7/2. Mr. Gould will file the accounting on what she has collected and what is still out there at least 15 days before 7/2 and it can be set for hearing on 7/2. No I&A is needed. Mr. Mori will file a status report on what they have done with the taxes and what is needed to repair the house. He can file an exparte petition on the repair.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Gena Eales CSR# 9176. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Thompson.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-16-2006 Continued To Apr-02-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-16-2006 Continued To Apr-02-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Called with case number 205402. Paul Mori appearing for and with co-executors Peter and Willima Naughton. Stephen Gould appearing for and with special administrator Debra Dolch. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. Status on marshalling assets and eschated assets reported by Mr. Gould. Ms. Dolch has submitted claim for the German assets. Request to have Ms. Dolch continued as the Special Administrator until the estate is restored. William Naughton wants to take over after the recovery of the German assets. Status on the house reported by Mr. Mori. The co-executors has came to an agreement to clean up the house in three weeks and the repair will begin. Court wants Ms. Dolch to remain as special administrator for 6 months to tie up all the loose end. She will do her final accounting and the co-executors can close the estate. Mr. Gould requests to have William Naughton go to the safe deposit box and get the certificates to give to Ms. Dolch to file the claims. Court is directing counsel and everyone to work together to go to the safe deposit box. Tax issues addressed. Mr. Gould has not seen any tax return for the last 30 years. Mr. Mori was informed by William Naughton that there were tax return filed. He does not know what they are. Ms. Dolch wants to start the process with the tax agency. Court is authorizing Ms. Dolch to work with the tax agency. Mr. Mori will share informations that he has with Mr. Gould. Matter is continued to 4/2/07 for status. The petitions in the Helen Naughton Estate is also continued to 4/2/07. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Patty Hubble CSR#3058. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-19-2006 Continued To Aug-16-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Called with case numbers 205402 and 288206. Anne Marie Paolini-Mori appearing for and with co-executors in William Naughton estate and for petitioner in the Helen Naughton estate. Stephen Gould appearing for and with Debra Dolch. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. Ms. Paolini-Mori agrees to suspension of the co-executors' powers over personal property and appointing Debra Dolch as special administrator for both estates to handle the German claims. This matter is continued to 8/16 for status. Court wants a status report from the co-executors on the eschated property and real property. Order appointing special administrator signed. Letters will expire until further order of court. Court will issue order consolidating 288206 with 205402. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. ADA Reporter: Patty Hubble. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-15-2006 Continued To Jun-19-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Case called with case no. 205402 and case no. 288206. Stephen Gould appearing for Debra Dolch. Anne Marie Paolini-Mori appearing for and with co-executors William Naughton and Peter Naughton. Elizabeth Moore, Peter Naughton's daughter is also in court. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. Peter Naughton is a contractor and he will bring the property to code. Court is consolidating the two William Naughton cases into the lower number 205402. Ms. Dolch to remain as the Special Administator in both the William and Helen estates to continue to pursue the claim in Germany. Her appointment will expire until further order of court. Mr. Ferdon states his concerns regarding the non action by William Naughton for a long period. Court is allowing the co-executors to continue to be the personal representatives. Court wants a status report to be filed by 6/12. Status is set for hearing on 6/19. Ms. Paolini-Mori will have to give notice of the status report to the beneficiary in Germany. She informs the court that she may be able to get a waiver from the beneficiary. If the court determines that the co-executors are acting sufficiently, they will be removed and Ms. Dolch will be appointed on 6/19. Mr. Ferdon objects to Ms. Dolch's appointment. The Public Administrator asserts their priority. Ms. Dolch's petition for appointment in both estates is continued to 6/19. Mr. Naughton's petition in Helen's estate set for hearing on 5/30 is also continued to 6/19. Mr. Gould will submit the language that he wishes to include in the order consolidating cases. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722 and Patty Hubble ADA Court Reporter. Clerk: Ella Yip.
